skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem:
y = 4x − 5

One of the two equations in a system of linear equations is given. The system has infinitely many solutions. Which equation could be the second equation in this system?
rationale: For infinitely many solutions, the second equation must be equivalent to the first. Choice B is 3 times each side of y = 4x − 5, so it reduces to the same equation. The correct answer is choice B.
